name: Indian English Literary Narrator
voice_prompt: Perfect audio quality. Narrator with educated Indian English accent—the refined English of someone raised bilingual, not the exaggerated 'call center' stereotype. Male or female, middle-aged. Musical quality to phrasing, slightly more formal register than American casual. Warm, intelligent, capable of both humor and gravity. Comfortable with Hindi/Sanskrit terms pronounced correctly.
notes: Think: the voice of Arundhati Roy or Salman Rushdie reading their own work. Accent should feel natural and educated, not performed. Hindi/Sanskrit terms should be pronounced correctly, not Anglicized.
